text.title: Gce Spot Vms Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gce-spot-vms methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Preemption wipes hours of work; full re-execution required.
antipattern.root-cause: Worker doesn't write progress to durable storage.
antipattern.fix: Add checkpoint at intervals shorter than expected preemption window (e.g. every 5 min).

antipattern.symptom: Uncommitted work lost on 30s shutdown notice; downstream state corrupts.
antipattern.fix: Trap SIGTERM, flush buffers, persist state, exit clean.

antipattern.symptom: Full preemption wipes the fleet; outage during preemption storms.
antipattern.fix: Add non-Spot baseline (≥1 instance) for SLO floor.

antipattern.symptom: SLO unattainable; on-call pages due to preemption.
antipattern.root-cause: Workload with >99.5% SLO running on Spot only.
antipattern.fix: Move to standard VMs OR raise SLO commitment.
